seoTitle: How to Export a Power BI Report to PowerPoint Fast
metaDescription: Learn how to export a Power BI report to PowerPoint quickly, making it easier to share data-driven slides in meetings and client presentations.
intro: Not every audience wants to interact with a live Power BI report — sometimes a straightforward PowerPoint deck is what a meeting actually calls for. This guide covers how to export a Power BI report to PowerPoint so you can share your data visualizations as slides. It's a handy skill for anyone who regularly presents Power BI insights to teams or clients.

steps.heading: Navigate to the export option
steps.body: Open your report in Power BI Desktop or the Power BI service and find the Export option, typically located in the File or ribbon menu.

steps.heading: Export to PowerPoint
steps.body: Select the PowerPoint export format, choosing whether to export current view or all report pages as individual slides.

steps.heading: Customize your slides
steps.body: Once exported, open the file in PowerPoint to adjust formatting, add speaker notes, or rearrange slides to fit your presentation flow.

steps.heading: Share insights with your team
steps.body: Distribute the finished PowerPoint file through your normal channels, whether that's email, a shared drive, or a live presentation.

faq.question: Do exported slides stay interactive like the live Power BI report?
faq.answer: No, exported PowerPoint slides are static images of your visuals, so any filtering or drill-down interactivity is lost in that format.

faq.question: Will the exported slides automatically update if my data changes?
faq.answer: No, the export captures a snapshot at that point in time, so you'll need to re-export whenever you want the slides to reflect updated data.

faq.question: Can I export just one page of my report instead of the whole thing?
faq.answer: Yes, most export options let you choose between exporting the current page only or all pages in the report as separate slides.

faq.question: Is there a way to keep some interactivity when presenting from Power BI instead?
faq.answer: Yes, presenting directly from the Power BI service preserves full interactivity, so exporting to PowerPoint is best reserved for when a static deck is actually needed.
